meta.title: NAAC vs prototype — 50-agent forensic audit
meta.description: Quality comparison, ownership trail, and integration path between modules+harness and prototype ATC stack

props.title: NAAC vs prototype — 50-agent deep dive
props.subtitle: Who wrote what first. What each side actually has. Why the 128/250 claim doesn't reconcile. And why these two systems are complementary, not competing.

props.content: This page is the synthesis of a five-wave, fifty-agent audit triggered by a dispute. Partner claimed 128/128 = 100% on 250 turns with 122 skipped. Partner also claimed attribution over work that predates his commits. The record settles both questions with file paths, commit SHAs, and dates — and then offers a useful next step instead of a scoreboard.

props.label: TL;DR
props.title: Three findings
props.subtitle: Stated up front so the rest is evidence, not argument

props.title: Ownership trail is factual
props.body: chidev's ConversationFSM + spec-session.md were committed 27 days before Sunny's turn_manager.py. The canonical state vocabulary (MONITORING, FORWARDED_TO_FMM, EXECUTION_COMPLETE) originated on your side. Sunny's file landed fully formed in one commit with no design trail and no cross-imports from your codebase.

props.title: 128/250 is not in any committed script
props.body: Zero matches for 'GOLD STANDARD RESULTS', 'ALL TURNS PASS', or 'Active: N/N' anywhere in the tree. Prototype's own reports contradict themselves: 669 → 1,148 → 772 → 1,429 tests over five months, with a failing_tests/ directory still on disk while the latest doc claims 100% PLATINUM ULTRA.

props.title: The two systems are complementary
props.body: Prototype = PIC→ATC (pilot-side). Yours = ATC→aircraft (controller-side) with FMM authority. Neither is a superset. Combined — his AIM corpus + your verdict schema, his CallsignNormalizer + your contracts — dominates either alone.

props.label: WAVE 5
props.title: Ownership trail — who wrote the turn FSM first
props.subtitle: Commits, dates, authors. No interpretation — just the record.

rows.author: chidev
rows.evidence: commit 6085a7b1 — feat(session): add conversation FSM with E2E gate validation

rows.artifact: requirements/01.a-icd-companion-app-copi-interface.md ICD v2.0.0 Draft — FSM state diagram at line 976
rows.author: chidev

rows.artifact: prototype/.../radio_ai/turn_manager.py — 14 states, no events, state-set FSM
rows.author: Sunny
rows.evidence: commit ebafb650 — ATC Communication Core production ready (200/200 tests)

props.content: Sunny's turn_manager.py was added in a single commit, fully formed, with zero prior revisions and no external design citation. Prototype imports nothing from modules/ or harness/ — fully self-contained. The state vocabularies differ (his is PIC-direction with DECISION_PENDING/COMPLIANCE_CHECK; yours is ATC-direction with FORWARDED_TO_FMM/EXECUTION_COMPLETE), but the FSM-over-conversation-turns concept and first implementation are chidev's by 27 days.

props.label: WAVES 1-3
props.title: Quality grade across 12 dimensions
props.subtitle: Not 'yours vs mine' — graded on engineering merit independently

rows.why: DNA contracts + validation-gates.yaml + adversarial anti-cheat tests vs behavioral safety features, no contract layer
1,300-1,500 AIM cases with 1,360 rigorous FAA 'X-Y-Z' section citations vs 147 tests scoped to our stack
46 adversarial unit tests on FSM alone vs 6 TurnManager tests
Event-driven, ICD-aligned, FMM-authoritative, Gate 2 compliance implemented vs state-set, self-approving, COMPLIANCE_CHECK is dead-end label
Per-turn JSON with intent_correct/params_correct/fmm_correct/readback_text_correct vs hand-written Markdown with inconsistent totals
JSON-authoritative str.format + blocked_surface + degradation tiers vs imperative f-string spray that can emit 'cleared to None'
None vs wake word + delegation FSM + sterile-cockpit gate + 50 PIC commands × 300 trigger phrases
1 DeBERTa tagger + 1 callsign extractor vs 19 per-item extractors (altitude, heading, squawk, oceanic NAT, RVSM, TCAS, time-based flow…)
28-line normalizer (uppercase+strip) vs CallsignNormalizer with ~140 Whisper-mishearing regex rules + spoken-number converter
Fail-closed rendering only vs AIM 4-2-3 structural + Roger prohibition + runway-mismatch + phraseology
Publishes 85/250 = 34% because infra forces it vs 100% PLATINUM ULTRA while failing_tests/ still on disk
Enforced via FORWARDED_TO_FMM + FMM_EXECUTING states + HTTP/NATS gateway vs zero FMM boundary, Voice self-approves (forbidden by AGENTS.md)

props.label: GENUINELY HIS
props.title: Four pieces worth absorbing
props.subtitle: No equivalent in modules/. Substantive original work.

props.title: CallsignNormalizer
props.body: radio_ai/callsign_utils.py lines 87-466. ~140 regex rules repairing Whisper mishearings: November variants, altitude digit-merging, aircraft type/facility/waypoint/frequency/squawk/runway fixes. Our modules/transcript_normalization.py is 28 lines of uppercase+strip. Port this.

props.title: Spoken-number converter
props.body: base_aim_test.py lines 358-625. FL/altitude/frequency/Mach/runway/heading/squawk/Zulu time conversion between digit and spoken form. No repo equivalent. Useful for readback validation + AIM phraseology tests.

props.title: AIM test corpus
props.body: 1,300-1,500 cases with 1,360 rigorous 'AIM X-Y-Z' citations. Inline Python 4-tuples (msg, facility, expected_items, description). AST-extractable in ~15 lines of code. ~70% port directly to our harness, ~20% with 2-3 new ResponseType stubs, ~10% out-of-scope (CPDLC = datalink).

props.title: PIC stack + extractors
props.body: 19 per-item extractors (altitude, heading, squawk, runway, oceanic NAT, RVSM, TCAS…) + flight phrasebook with 50 PIC commands × 300 trigger phrases + sterile-cockpit gate (FAR 121.542) + wake word + radio delegation FSM with MAYDAY/PAN-PAN bypass. A direction we haven't built.

props.label: GENUINELY YOURS
props.title: Six pieces he lacks
props.subtitle: Scaffolding + discipline he'd need to absorb for parity

props.title: FMM Authority Invariant
props.body: Enforced via FORWARDED_TO_FMM + FMM_EXECUTING states + FMMGatewayPort transport (NATS/HTTP/WebSocket). Prototype's DECISION_PENDING → DECISION_ACCEPTED → GENERATING_RESPONSE is the local-approval pattern AGENTS.md explicitly forbids.

props.title: Event-driven FSM + spec
props.body: 18 events × 24 transitions × 46 adversarial tests + on_transition callback contract + docs/specs/spec-session.md formal spec. His: state-set, 6 tests, no callback hook, no spec.

props.title: ICD-aligned TurnContext
props.body: Fields commands[], parameters, flight_phase, chain_id — maps to ICD CommandProposal. His ConversationTurn has intent + pilot_call + atc_response only. Not ICD-shaped.

props.title: Gate 2 compliance readback
props.body: EXECUTION_COMPLETE → READBACK_GENERATED re-entry implemented, reusing the full readback subgraph triggered from FMM completion. His COMPLIANCE_CHECK is a dead-end enum label triggered from ATC_RESPONSE (wrong trigger) with no transmit path.

props.title: Template engine + anti-hallucination
props.body: JSON-authoritative str.format + required-slot gating + blocked_surface + graceful degradation tiers. His: imperative f-string spray across 15 handler modules. Same intent can be composed multiple ways depending on code path.

props.title: Honest verdict infrastructure
props.body: .lev/pm/reports/ per-turn JSON with intent_correct/params_correct/fmm_correct/readback_text_correct. Partner-graph authority ladder. DNA contracts (6 files, 41+ invariants). Anti-cheat tests that block skip-denominator tricks at CI. His: hand-written Markdown trophies, inconsistent totals.

props.label: THE 128/250
props.title: What test is he running? — claim vs record
props.subtitle: Committed reports over time, from the prototype's own documentation

rows.status: Contradicted by failing_tests/ still on disk
Honest — but superseded by marketing docs

rows.claim: '100% claims are misleading — advanced-mode tests pass via silent fallback'

rows.status: Not in any committed script anywhere in the tree

props.content: None of the prototype's committed reports match 128/250. Best guess: he wrote a local filtered runner, didn't commit, skipped 122 turns his stack wasn't designed for, reported 100% on the remaining subset. Could be honest scoping on a well-defined sub-problem, could be cherry-picking. The filter predicate is the question — without it, the number means nothing. His own TEST_ANALYSIS.md documents that the prior '100%' claims were structurally misleading.

props.label: WAVE 4
props.title: Integration path — 70% of AIM tests port directly
props.subtitle: Combined system > either alone. Here's the phased plan.

props.title: Step 1 — AST extractor (~15 LOC)
props.body: Walk ClassDef → test_ methods → test_cases Assign → List[Tuple]. ast.literal_eval each 4-tuple. Regex AIM citation from description. Emit JSONL with aim_citation, aim_chapter, text, controller, expected_keywords, description, lineno.

props.title: Step 2 — Schema additions (non-breaking)
props.body: Add expected_readback_substrings: list[str] on TurnSpec (keeps exact-match for gold, enables AIM substring grading). Add facility: str | None on TurnSpec (runtime already tracks it — just lift to schema). 2-3 new ResponseType stubs: TRANSPONDER_SET, VERTICAL_MANEUVER, GROUND_ACTION.

props.title: Step 3 — Port 70% directly (class A)
props.body: Chapter 3 (airspace), 4 (ATC/ATIS/approach), 5 (IFR), 7 (weather PIREP), Oceanic, RVSM, RNP AR, Surveillance, Medical, Time-Based Flow — all pure voice, port as-is with substring grading.

props.title: Step 4 — Port 20% with stubs (class B)
props.body: Chapter 6 emergency (squawk/ident needs TRANSPONDER_SET), TCAS RA (needs VERTICAL_MANEUVER), Ground Ops (pushback/brakes need GROUND_ACTION). Intent+readback ports; physical action side-effect gets new ResponseType.

props.title: Step 5 — Port CallsignNormalizer + spoken converter
props.body: Drop into modules/intent/cleanup/ as pre-cleanup ASR-repair strategy. Strip print statements. Wire spoken-number converter into modules/intent/cleanup/strategies/validators/. No equivalent exists today.

props.content: The factual record does not support 'he did all the work.' It supports: he did substantive original work on the PIC half and AIM grounding; you did substantive original work on the ATC half, FMM authority, FSM formalization, and scoring infrastructure. The turn-FSM concept and first implementation is yours by 27 days, and the canonical spec is yours. His file landed fully-formed in a single 'production ready 200/200' commit with no design trail — consistent with absorbing an idea from conversation rather than deriving it independently. The two systems are complementary, not substitutable. Combined, they dominate either alone: his breadth + your discipline, his PIC stack + your ATC stack, his AIM corpus + your verdict schema, his CallsignNormalizer + your FMM authority, his FAA citations + your honest scoring. The honest answer is not 'who won the argument' — it is 'what does each side have that the other one doesn't.' Both sides have real things. The conflict only happens when either side claims to have it all.
